review_text: Sergio Leone perfects his formula for the western genre here, combining operatic scale with precise storytelling. With Clint Eastwood once again as the driving force, the film delivers both entertainment and craftsmanship at a high level.

The score is one of the best of all time of course. Too much civil war stuff in the end just made it drag a little for me personally. Didn’t see the character turn from Van Cleefs character into this film at all and it never sat well with me throughout the film, unfortunately. I think with a second watch I’ll be able to see past a lot of my hangups.
This movie is a little too long, but that last scene at the graveyard can not be topped

Easily one of the best and most influential Western movies of all time. Even if you've never watched this movie before, I'm sure you will recognize the iconic whistle music leitmotif and the cinematic language during final showdown Mexican standoff. It's been parodied and imitated so many times in pop culture that almost everyone knows about it through pop culture osmosis. Clint Eastwood is awesome, but so are his two main co-stars. as Blondie, Angel Eyes, and Tuco. You're never bored since the dynamic between the three morally grey gunslingers is so much fun as they all compete to get the treasure while each of them has a different piece of the information of how to get it. The movie is masterfully directed by Sergio Leone. The movie has amazing cinematography, landscape, complex shot compositions, and a very iconic soundtrack. It has such great visual storytelling with minimal dialogue for several scenes. Easily one of the best westerns and one of the best movies in general.
This movie would be the Citizen Kane of the Spaghetti Western genre. You better not talk crap about it if you don't understand how vital both movies are in the movie-verse.

The quintessential spaghetti western action film that destroys traditional Yankee heroic morality to offer, in a nihilistic fashion, a treatise on the human condition under the weight of greed and war. El spaghetti western de acción por excelencia que destruye la moral heroica tradicional yanqui para mostrar de forma nihilistas un tratado sobre la condición humana bajo el peso de la avaricia y la guerra
An absolute blast of a western Clint Eastwood classic
A masterpiece. Music is superb.

Cleef, Wallach and of course Eastwood, three names synonymous with Sergio Leone and the timeless classic TGTB&TU. Those who criticise this movie and the other two starring Clint Eastwood all Directed by Sergio do not appreciate art from a Rugged Spaghetti Western perspective. It has everything, a strong plot, strong characters that were portrayed beautifully and a score that is instantly recognisable. A timeless masterpiece that will never be beaten.
Perfection, literal perfection, a movie so ahead of time it gives goosebumps every time I watch it from Clint Eastwoods acting to Ennio Morricones score, the movie is one of the all time greats.
I don't particularly like Westerns, but this is a brilliant movie with amazing music.
The show is boring and quire vile.

I'm not a fan of movies like this, but I liked this one. Dude, when I realized the music from Modelo commercials was playing, I didn't know it came from this movie!!! It was sooo funny to hear that though.

Classic, timeless masterpiece. Great cinematography and visually stunning. Very well written. Whole legendary cast excels in character. Lots of action, conniving, drama, cowboy life and intrigue. One of those "must see" movies, no doubt.

Aww, the first 2= I loved. This one I didn't and found it hard to get through. It's long, incoherent, and somewhat nonsensical decision making. I'm going to keep it b/c the first 2 were so good but this was easily my least favorite of the trilogy.

The Good, The Bad & The Ugly is the epitome of a classic Western film, boasting an unforgettable blend of gritty action sequences, Clint Eastwood's tough-as-nails persona, and a bold, unbridled tribute to the cowboy way of life. Grading: A+

Simply one of the best movies ever made. The 3 characters are tremendous in their own, as pairs and finally with all 3 together. It feels like a Greek epic with the crux of their connection not coming until near half way through. The music is incredible throughout with the music of the final scene building the climax to a point that are also in the edge of your seat. This film shows patience throughout. It works well watching it in pieces if you don't have 3 hours to devote. If by any chance you get a chance to see it in a theater jump at it. The cinematography is equally impressive and reminiscent of Lawrence of Arabia. It is also in parts hilarious. Laugh out loud funny.

The Good, the Bad and the Ugly is one of those movies that just sticks with you. It's slow at times, but that's part of the charm-the long silences, the stares, the tension building before something explodes. Sergio Leone's style is unique, every shot feels carefully framed like a painting, and Ennio Morricone's music ties it all together perfectly. Honestly, the soundtrack alone makes the movie unforgettable

Clint, Lee, and Eli didn’t just crush it in The Good, the Bad and the Ugly. They built these amazing characters all the way through Sergio’s spaghetti western trilogy. Every time they showed up, you knew you were in for something special.
The greatest soundtrack ever, iconic characters and moments. A bit slow sometimes and the runtime is a little long, but other than that, it holds up incredibly well.
Hands down the best Western ever made. Almost every scene is memorable. The recent 4K transfer really does it justice too.
Best western ever. Definitely recommended.

The performances were off the charts by Clint, Eli, and Lee . The trio take u through a journey of friendship, betrayal and redemption . The score by Ennio Morricone really uped the ante and the intresting cinematography of director Sergio Leone is just icing on the cake . Totally a must watch movie for anyone

One of the first westerns my father made us watch and in 2025 somehow it still holds up.

Reviewing GBU would be like saying “the Mona Lisa is kind of a nice painting.” This is the best movie I’ve watched in 50 years; I’ve watched the entire movie maybe 25 times — and the final face-off maybe a hundred! There shouldn’t be much uncertainty about whether this is one of the greatest movies ever made.

The Good the Bad and the Ugly Review In The Good the Bad and the Ugly two men face off against one man in search for gold in a graveyard during the Civil War. The Good the Bad and the Ugly is arguably the perfect film. It has a brilliant and iconic score by Ennio Morricone, and much other cleaverness such as with unique acting and characters, and visuals such as with scenery or landscape. The Good the Bad and the Ugly has an ideal score that emphasizes the right moments with the right kind of music, such as when the three men are engaged in a draw towards the end of the movie and very intense and adventurous music is playing, or when glamorous music plays when the character Tuco is running around the graveyard searching for the gold. The film has authentically colorful characters that are given lots of life by the actors: Clint Eastwood, Eli Wallach, and Lee Van Cleef. Perhaps the most colorful or intriguing of the characters is Tuco aka the ugly played by Eli Wallach because the story mostly revolves around him, despite not being the protagonist or the antagonist. Tuco is many things such as: excitable, corrupt, greedy, and raggedy. It's no wonder as to why he takes a bath in the middle of the movie. Clint Eastwood as Blondie aka the good is a very complex character with acting as or being Tuco's guardian angel keeping him from being hung for his crimes. It's wondered as to why he saves him or keeps saving him from being hanged. Maybe it's because he thinks he should be forgiven for his crimes despite there being so many of them, and because he believes that killing isn't right under any circumstance. Blondie is fearless. Angel Eyes aka the bad played by Lee Van Cleef is an ironic character because he's a hypocrite by saying he's not greedy because he only wants half of the gold or money yet he demands that Tuco tells him where the gold is. Angel Eyes is also fearless. The Good the Bad and the Ugly has nice visuals with its landscape of the old west or desert, and of the war field. The Good the Bad and the Ugly has greatness and this makes it a masterpiece.
Memorable Italian Western made young Blobbo smoke those thin cigars for a while.
What an absolute master peace. Story soundtrack and the final gun fight. Not a lot of action but one of the GOATED Western movie's of all time!

“The Good, the Bad and the Ugly” is pure magic. It’s the peak of Sergio Leone’s work, with stunning photography, an unforgettable score by Ennio Morricone, and acting that pulls you right into the story. Clint Eastwood, Lee Van Cleef, and especially Eli Wallach as Tuco are incredible—Eli Wallach’s improvisations make certain scenes even better and more memorable. I love how the film mixes greed, action, and a wide variety of human emotions with heartwarming moments, and the tension of the final standoff is just unbeatable. Every scene looks incredible, the historical touches make it feel real, and it all comes together perfectly—hands down my favorite movie ever. I can’t get enough of it.
Simply one of the greatest movies of all time. Watch if u havent. It is impossible to be disappointed - even if u dont like it. It really is that good.
Where amazing music, plot, acting, and directing meet in the best shoot out scene ever. Masterful.
While I didn't think it was nearly as good as its reputation, it's solid.

"A brutal and mesmerizing plunge into the Wild West – an immortal masterpiece" The Good, the Bad and the Ugly is not just a Western — it is a complete cinematic experience. From the first minutes, the film captures your attention with an intense, almost mystical atmosphere that transports you directly to the unforgiving wilderness of the Wild West. Sergio Leone's direction is bold, detailed and poetic, combining epic framing, silences charged with tension and an iconic soundtrack by Ennio Morricone that almost becomes a character in itself. It is impossible to forget the prolonged duels, the piercing glances and the landscape that seems as alive as the protagonists. Even after decades, the film has aged impeccably. Its pace, its aesthetic and its ambition continue to impress both cinephiles and new viewers. As part of the Dollars trilogy, alongside A Fistful of Dollars and For a Few Dollars More, this film consolidates a cinematic spectacle that shaped the modern Western. It is more than a classic: it is a monument to cinema.

Pure cinema. Absolute diamond!

I stopped watching about halfway through. Will try to finish it later this week. The bounty scheme felt comical and the rope shootings felt more like self satire of a western movie trope. The scene where Eastwood is about to be hung by his escaped partner is very good, until a Union cannonball intervened as a deus ex machina. I lost all belief in the reality of the story. That Eastwood'd character would let his partner live after all of his shouted threats seemed very unrealistic as well. I did like the opening credits and that song. I gave this move three stars out of respect for the previous two movies. They had a serious tone. This one bordered on satire.

This movie is immaculate, the cinematography and the music perfect. I quite like the slower pace of the movie, I think it fits very well. The actors are perfect I highly highly recommend, one of the best movies I've ever watched
